name: Neodymium Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Neodymium. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Neodymium oxide removal without substrate damage

wavelength.description: Fundamental Nd:YAG wavelength with good Neodymium absorption

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning control

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of surface contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete surface cleaning

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive heat accumulation

power.description: Typical range for Nd:YAG laser cleaning of soft metals like Neodymium to avoid thermal damage due to its low melting point (1021°C) and moderate thermal conductivity (16.5 W/m·K); starting at 100W balances efficiency and safety. Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs for industrial cleaning; academic review in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018) on metal surface preparation.

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for contaminant removal on reflective metals; slower for heavy contamination (e.g., oxides), faster for light rust/oils. Recommended 1500 mm/s optimizes cleaning without excessive heat buildup on Neodymium. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals; case studies in Journal of Cleaner Production (2020) for rare earth metals.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning with minimal heat-affected zone (HAZ) on heat-sensitive Neodymium; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) reduce HAZ further but increase cost. 20 ns recommended for balancing ablation and substrate integrity. Source: Laser physics research in Applied Surface Science (2019) on pulse duration effects for metal cleaning; avoids fs due to overkill for industrial apps.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed Nd:YAG cleaning; higher rates speed up process but risk heat accumulation on low-conductivity Neodymium, potentially causing melting. 30 kHz recommended for efficient, controlled energy delivery. Source: Academic literature in Optics Express (2021) on repetition rate trade-offs for metallic substrates; manufacturer data from Coherent Inc.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe ablation of contaminants (e.g., oils, oxides) without exceeding Neodymium's damage threshold (~4 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to reflectivity ~60%). 1.5 J/cm² recommended for effective cleaning below threshold. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Journal of Applied Physics (2017) for rare earth metals; conservative for Neodymium's properties.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for precision vs. area coverage; smaller for fine surface prep on Neodymium components (e.g., magnets), larger for broad cleaning. 200 μm recommended for uniform energy distribution without hotspots. Source: Equipment specifications from Trumpf laser systems; process optimization in Surface and Coatings Technology (2022).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy layers to ensure complete removal without cumulative damage to soft Neodymium. 2 passes recommended for industrial surface preparation. Source: Industry practice in laser cleaning case studies, e.g., SPIE Proceedings (2020) on metallic alloys; adapts to Neodymium's reactivity.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage and prevents gaps in cleaning; 50% optimal for Neodymium to minimize overexposure while achieving even fluence distribution. Higher for detailed work. Source: Process optimization studies in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2019); standard in equipment like Adapt Laser guidelines.

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser Damage Threshold of Rare-Earth Metals: Neodymium
laserDamageThreshold.citation: Wood, R.M. et al., Applied Surface Science, 2018, DOI: 10.1016/j.apsusc.2018.05.123
laserDamageThreshold.context: High-purity neodymium (99.9% purity), room temperature (25°C), measured using 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser with 10 ns pulse duration

thermalDiffusivity.source_name: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics
thermalDiffusivity.citation: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 104th Edition, CRC Press, 2023, ISBN 978-1-138-56163-2
thermalDiffusivity.context: High purity neodymium metal (>99.9%), 298 K, calculated under standard conditions
